question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked zero didactic questions. It immediately delivered a complete solution with explanation and never engaged the user with a choice, despite the project's stated two-option paradigm offering obvious teachable forks (set vs. all-loop, ascii_lowercase vs. literal string, filtering non-letters).

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all. The alternative one-liner was presented as a stylistic footnote rather than as a substantive trade-off question for the user.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no sequence to scaffold — a single monolithic answer with the full solution and even a hint that issubset is 'more Pythonic,' which would spoil any pedagogical fork had one existed.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and friendly with a tidy explanation and test table, but there is no back-and-forth; the user simply rubber-stamps the solution. Functional but not pedagogical.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
